title: BriefForgeInput
description: Workflow input for brief-forge. Per stacked-dev convention, every loop cap is a REQUIRED input — the workflow bakes in no defaults.

task_statement.description: The finding/task in full scope text. Every claim in it is a hypothesis until the scout looks.

task_ref.description: Ledger/task ID the resulting brief will discharge.

related_refs.description: Related context as pasted SCOPE TEXT, never bare task IDs — the agents may not share a ledger with the composer.

refute_cap.description: Max design→refute rounds before surfacing to the operator as a contested design space.

diagnose_only.description: Stop after a diagnosis-complete brief; dispatch deferred deliberately.

base.description: The landed base the stack sits on (usually main's tip at stack creation).

summary.description: One line: what this unlanded branch changes.

diff_ref.description: Path to the branch's captured diff-vs-parent, when available.

stack.description: Present when the work grounds on an unlanded yggdrasil stack. Passed verbatim to the scout and designer: the scout must distinguish main-landed code from code introduced in unlanded branches, and the designer may route a fix as an AMENDMENT to the unlanded branch that introduced the defect (cheap while unlanded) instead of stacking a fix on top of it.
